user_message: Just got this from Angela Doyle at Contoso Ltd -- I need help figuring out QBR preparation. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 17:37 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 12:35 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-28 13:18 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Contoso Ltd: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-A trap (Loses a commitment buried in a side thread); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-A failure mode (Loses a commitment buried in a side thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-A -- Loses a commitment buried in a side thread
